PFE.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q1 2026 earnings on May 5, 2026 — approximately 4 trading days out. Net news sentiment: negative/mixed. EPS consensus implies ~19.6% YoY decline ($0.72-0.77), Metsera GLP-1 acquisition widely characterized as 'playing catch-up' vs. Lilly/Novo, FDA issued a misleading-ads warning on Adcetris, and the $21.4B patent cliff is a structural headwind. Partial offset: full-year guidance was reaffirmed ($2.80-$3.00 adj. EPS). Insider activity: CEO Bourla has been buying shares — a genuine bullish signal, but size undisclosed and insufficient to overcome the bearish news flow. Technical setup going in: poor. Daily price ($26.26) is below EMA-5 through EMA-50, MACD histogram deteriorating (-0.1247), ADX-DI configuration bearish (-DI 28.87 vs +DI 15.72), 5/10/21-day momentum all negative (ROC: -2.01%, -3.42%, -5.44%). RSI-7 at 17.19 is deeply oversold, creating a potential bounce risk, but oversold alone is not a constructive pre-catalyst setup — it is a downtrend. Hourly shows modest recovery (OBV trending up, relative volume 1.35x) but ADX hourly downtrend alignment is set. Pre-catalyst support at ~$26.20 (recent low) and $26.38 (hourly pivot). Resistance at $26.91 then $27.67. With the chart deteriorating into a binary print that already has a bearish consensus EPS read, there is no constructive technical base — the catalyst is approaching but the setup does not meet the entry criteria. Neutral: catalyst exists but setup is not constructive going in.

XOM.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q1 2026 earnings on May 1, 2026 — approximately 1 trading day out. Net news sentiment: mixed with a complex offsetting structure. Bearish: U.S.-Iran ceasefire (Apr 10) collapsed the geopolitical risk premium (~14% Brent decline), Hormuz closure cut ~6% of Q1 global output, downstream timing headwinds of $3.3-5.3B, a $600-800M one-time impairment, and OPEC cutting Q2 demand forecast. Bullish: upstream earnings boosted ~$2.9B from elevated Q1 oil prices, CFO characterizes downstream timing effects as temporary and reversible, Uaru/Guyana production growth intact, $2B Pioneer synergies realized, lithium diversification launched. Analyst consensus is strong buy with average targets of $154-159 — essentially at current price — so even a beat may not provide meaningful upside surprise. Insider activity: none reported across all run history — quiet. Technical setup: daily chart constructive (price above short-term MAs, OBV up, RSI-14 neutral at 49.33, 5-day ROC +3.46%), but hourly shows warning flags: RSI-21 at 73.48 (overbought), OBV diverging bearishly from price, price at pct_b 0.8638 (near upper Bollinger), and immediate resistance at $155.89. The sharp 3-day recovery from $148 to $154.67 has already priced in some optimism ahead of earnings. With earnings in 1 trading day, a mixed upstream beat / downstream miss print, analyst targets already at current price, and hourly overbought conditions with OBV divergence, the risk/reward is too symmetric to take a directional swing position. Neutral: setup is borderline constructive but catalyst risk is too binary and ambiguous given the complex offsetting forces.
